behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is template-lint logic itself (string/rule check). No real harness assembly, subprocess, network, or model call is required to expose leakage-detection regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative prompt-template strings into the lint function: one clean template and one containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md. Assert lint rejects the leaked case with a precise violation and accepts the clean case.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is cross-module: template + fixture interpolation + prompt assembly can reintroduce leaked instructions even if template lint passes. Must test assembled output from the real harness path.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-builder pipeline with production-like fixtures and interpolation. Capture the final assembled prompt text and assert no forbidden phase-instruction snippets/patterns are present.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries: file-backed skill loader, prompt construction, and invocation wiring. A unit test of one function would miss hard-coded fallback rules masking missing skill context.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test invocation through the real harness with actual plugin/skill files on disk. Assert loaded context includes expected skill-derived content and that no hard-coded phase rules are injected when skill content changes location/source.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real model behavior changing when skill context quality degrades. Mocked/model-free checks cannot prove the regression signal; correctness depends on actual LLM output quality and variability.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same user-style behavioral smoke test twice against a real model: baseline skill vs intentionally degraded/removed SKILL.md section. Assert degraded variant fails the behavioral assertion while baseline passes (with retry/threshold policy to handle stochasticity).

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Ordering bug is between components (model call result handling, artifact writer, parser). Need wired pipeline test to ensure capture occurs pre-parse, including malformed JSON paths.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the response-processing pipeline with a raw output sample (including malformed JSON case). Assert diagnostic artifact is written with exact raw text before parser is invoked, and artifact still exists when parsing fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is assertion logic quality: weak keyword matcher vs structural/schema assertions. Can be exposed with deterministic canned outputs without full harness or real model.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion helpers with fixed outputs: (a) prompt-echo text containing expected keywords but wrong structure, (b) correctly structured response. Assert keyword-only echo fails and schema/field-placement-valid response passes.
